messages.content:
I can’t run tests from here. I can, however, tell you exactly how to run “load test task 8” on your machine or in CI and what commands/flags to use. Brief reasoning: this environment has no access to your workstation/CI, so I can only provide instructions.

Tell me which runner/tool or CI you’re using (k6, Locust, JMeter, Artillery, a pytest-based load test, a script in package.json/Makefile, etc.) and the file name if you know it. Meanwhile, here are compact instructions for the common runners — pick the one that matches your project:

k6
- Install: brew install k6 (mac) or use container loadimpact/k6.
- Run: k6 run path/to/task8.js
- Example with env and output: k6 run -e TARGET_URL=https://api.example.com --out json=results-task8.json path/to/task8.js
- Docker: docker run --rm -i -v $(pwd):/scripts -w /scripts loadimpact/k6 run /scripts/path/to/task8.js

Locust (Python)
- Install: pip install locust
- Run headless: locust -f path/to/task8.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 5m --host=https://api.example.com --csv=task8
- See HTML: locust in web UI (default 8089) if not headless.

JMeter
- Run non-GUI: jmeter -n -t path/to/task8.jmx -l results-task8.jtl -e -o report-dir
- Convert or view report: open report-dir/index.html

Artillery
- Install: npm i -g artillery
- Run: artillery run path/to/task8.yml -o results-task8.json
- Generate report: artillery report results-task8.json

pytest-based load tests (pytest + some plugin)
- Run specific test file: pytest path/to/test_task8.py -q
- If using locust/async fixtures, follow project-specific README.

General checklist before running
- Start the service under test (local server, Docker compose, staging).
- Set required env vars/secrets (AUTH_TOKEN, TARGET_URL, DB credentials).
- Ensure test data/state (seed DB if needed).
- Ensure resource limits on your machine/CI are sufficient for the intended load.
- Capture output: save logs and result files (JSON/CSV/JTL) for post-analysis.

If you tell me which tool/file/CI job corresponds to “task 8”, I’ll give the exact command you should run and the recommended flags to collect results and logs.
